question: Does carry-forward deduplication by desire_key alone flatten legitimate distinct continuation threads, and if so, what is the smallest general cognitive fix that preserves richer continuity without reintroducing clutter?
notes: The goal is not project memory. The goal is to preserve newly discovered fields of care in a general way.

In the clean identity run, session reflect proposed specific new carry-forward items for distinct external surfaces, but they were skipped because an older broad bootstrap carry-forward item already used the same desire key.
This may explain why the cleaner run can discover surfaces like arcagi3 and fano, yet still struggle to keep more than one specific continuation alive at once.
Evaluate this against the elegance standard: fewer rules, more life; no project-specific logic.
